content.text:
Advanced Algorithmics and Complexity
Semester:
1  |
Type:
UEF11  |
VHS:
63h  |
Weekly hours:
4h30  |
Lecture:
1h30  |
Tutorial:
1h30  |
Lab:
1h30
Personal work VHS:
42h  |
Coefficient:
3  |
Credits:
5
Teaching Objectives
Understand the notions of algorithmic complexity (time, space)
Master advanced algorithm design techniques
Classify problems according to their difficulty (P, NP, NP-complete, etc.)
Apply exact and approximate methods to complex problems
Prerequisites:
Foundations in algorithmics and data structures
Course Content (21h)
1. Algorithmic Complexity
Asymptotic notations (Big O, Ω, Θ)
Best-case, worst-case, and average-case analysis
Recurrence equations
2. Advanced Data Structures and Complexity
Binary trees (review), AVL trees, Red-Black trees
Hash tables
3. Textual Algorithmics and Complexity
Pattern matching
Compression algorithms
4. Design Paradigms
Divide and conquer
Dynamic programming
Greedy algorithms
Probabilistic algorithms
5. Problem Classes
Decision and optimisation problems
Determinism vs non-determinism
NP-completeness and problem reduction
6. Advanced Algorithms
Parallel and distributed algorithms
Online algorithms
Randomised algorithms
Assessment
Final examination (in person): 60%
Continuous assessment (CC): 40% — tests, personal work
Bibliography
T. Cormen, C. Leiserson, R. Rivest & C. Stein (2022).
Introduction to Algorithms
. The MIT Press, 5th Edition.
P. Brass (2008).
Advanced Data Structures
. Cambridge University Press.
C. A. Shaffer (2010).
A Practical Introduction to Data Structures and Algorithm Analysis
. Virginia Tech.
Sedgewick, R., & Wayne, K. (2011).
Algorithms
(4th ed.). Addison-Wesley.
Kozen, D. C. (1991).
The Design and Analysis of Algorithms
. Springer.
Motwani, R., & Raghavan, P. (1995).
Randomized Algorithms
. Cambridge University Press.
S. Arora & B. Barak (2006).
Computational Complexity: A Modern Approach
. Cambridge University Press.
